f9bdc95 Select correct events when displaying a checkpoint as part of a session
102ef82 Show in progress changes on branch log view as well as repos view.
42fdc81 Introduce a new 2 column layout with checkpoints separate from chat logs
bb1713c Reworking the checkpoint view. Fixing performance issues
dc1f926 Show checkpoints starting from a given repo
8a54f37 Show planning text as part of thinking block
e8acd36 Improve branch view
d47d372 Refactor for multiple repos at a time. Also show a repos entrypoint in case you want to index a repo without any of your active sessions on it.
7bff41d Add a resume session button from the main screen
dc4131e Show files changed and diffs of code for checkpoints

content.text:
You're on `ParsingEntireLog` with two uncommitted changes:

1. **`server.ts`** â€” skip checkpoints with no associated log events (server-side filter)
2. **`SessionDetail.tsx`** â€” filter sidebar checkpoints to only those that have items in the `groups` map (client-side filter)

Both changes are filtering out empty/orphaned checkpoints. Do you want to continue building on this, commit these, or is there something specific you want to work on?

message.content: Create a README with simple installation instructions. Say that you first have to install the entire.io cli (link to the project), call `entire enable` in your repo. Then to run the app, just clone the repo, and call npm run start to run both the UI and the server. Finally, add the repo's local path on your machine to the "repos" tab in gossamer

content.content:
~
app
dist
node_modules
package-lock.json
package.json
prisma
prisma.config.ts
README.md
src
tsconfig.json
{
  "name": "claude-hooks-listener",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Gossamer: indexes Entire CLI logs and serves them via HTTP + WebSocket",
  "type": "module",
  "bin": {
    "gossamer-serve": "./dist/serve.js"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "build": "prisma generate && tsc",
    "serve": "node dist/serve.js",
    "start": "concurrently --names \"serve,app\" -c \"blue,green\" \"npm run serve\" \"cd app && npm run dev\"",
    "db:push": "DATABASE_URL=\"file:$HOME/.claude/hook-handler.db\" prisma db push",
    "db:studio": "prisma studio"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/adapter-better-sqlite3": "^7.4.2",
    "@prisma/client": "^7.4.2",
    "better-sqlite3": "^12.6.2",
    "cors": "^2.8.5",
    "express": "^4.18.2",
    "gitlog": "^5.1.0",
    "ws": "^8.18.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/better-sqlite3": "^7.6.13",
    "@types/cors": "^2.8.17",
    "@types/express": "^4.17.21",
    "@types/node": "^22.0.0",
    "@types/ws": "^8.5.13",
    "concurrently": "^9.2.1",
    "prisma": "^7.4.2",
    "typescript": "^5.0.0"
  }
}

   136â†’
   137â†’Built for developers who care about the work behind the work.
   138â†’

file.content:
# Gossamer

**Never lose the context behind your code again.**

Gossamer is a desktop control panel for your Claude Code sessions. It indexes, searches, and backs up every AI session you've ever run â€” so the thinking, the decisions, the rabbit holes, the breakthroughs â€” all of it is preserved and searchable forever.

And because Gossamer integrates with **[Entire.io](https://entire.io)**, your AI sessions are linked directly to your Git commits. Every line of code has a story. Now you can actually read it.

---

## What it does

- **Index** every Claude Code session automatically as you work
- **Search** across all your sessions â€” find that conversation where you figured out the auth bug three months ago
- **Back up** sessions so nothing is ever lost, even across machines
- **Link sessions to commits** via Entire.io â€” click any commit and see the full AI conversation behind it
- **Browse checkpoints** â€” step through the evolution of a session with diffs and context at every turn
- **Resume sessions** â€” jump back into past conversations right where you left off

---

## Requirements

- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org) v18+
- [Entire CLI](https://entire.io) installed and authenticated
- Claude Code installed and configured

> **Homebrew installs coming soon.** For now, clone and run locally.

---

## Getting started

### 1. Clone the repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-org/gossamer.git
cd gossamer
```

### 2. Install server dependencies and build

```bash
npm install
npm run build
```

### 3. Push the database schema

```bash
npm run db:push
```

This creates the SQLite database at `~/.claude/hook-handler.db`.

### 4. Install app dependencies

```bash
cd app && npm install && cd ..
```

### 5. Run

```bash
npm start
```

This launches both the backend server and the Electron desktop app together. The server indexes your sessions and exposes a local API; the Electron app is the UI that talks to it.

---

## Architecture

Gossamer has two components that run together:

| Component | What it does |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Server** (`src/serve.ts`) | Node.js + Express + WebSocket server. Indexes Claude Code sessions from disk, syncs with Entire.io, and serves a real-time API on `localhost:3000`. |
| **App** (`app/`) | Electron desktop app built with React + Mantine. Connects to the local server and renders your session history, search, checkpoints, and diffs. |

Both start with `npm start`.

---

## How the Entire.io integration works

Gossamer uses the Entire CLI to associate your Claude Code sessions with the Git commits they produced. When you make a commit while Claude Code is active, Entire captures the link between the session and the commit hash.

From Gossamer you can:
- See which session produced which commit
- Open a commit and read the full AI conversation that led to it
- Never wonder "why did we write it this way?" again

Make sure the Entire CLI is installed and you're logged in before starting Gossamer.

---

## Your data, your machine

Gossamer is built on a simple principle: your AI sessions are yours. No telemetry, no cloud sync, no third-party storage. Everything stays local by default.

- **Search index and database** are stored in a single SQLite file at `~/.claude/hook-handler.db` â€” on your machine, readable with any SQLite tool, deletable whenever you want.
- **Session history** is stored in your local Git repository. Push it to your own remote to back it up or share it across machines â€” on your terms, using infrastructure you already control.
- **The server is local.** Gossamer's backend runs on `localhost` and never opens an external connection. The Electron app talks only to that local server.

You decide what gets backed up, who has access, and when it gets deleted. Gossamer never touches your data without you.

---

## Development

To work on the server and app separately:

```bash
# Server only (hot-reloads with tsc --watch)
npm run serve

# App only (Electron + Vite HMR)
cd app && npm run dev
```

Database UI:

```bash
npm run db:studio
```

---

## Coming soon

- `brew install gossamer` â€” one-line install, no Node required
- Team sharing â€” share session history with your whole org

---

Built for developers who care about the work behind the work.

content.text: The README already exists. I'll rewrite the Getting Started section to match your instructions â€” install Entire CLI, `entire enable`, clone, `npm run start`, then add the repo path in the Repos tab.

input.old_string:
## Getting started

### 1. Clone the repo

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-org/gossamer.git
cd gossamer
```

### 2. Install server dependencies and build

```bash
npm install
npm run build
```

### 3. Push the database schema

```bash
npm run db:push
```

This creates the SQLite database at `~/.claude/hook-handler.db`.

### 4. Install app dependencies

```bash
cd app && npm install && cd ..
```

### 5. Run

```bash
npm start
```

This launches both the backend server and the Electron desktop app together. The server indexes your sessions and exposes a local API; the Electron app is the UI that talks to it.
input.new_string:
## Getting started

### 1. Install the Entire CLI and enable it in your repo

Install the [Entire CLI](https://entire.io), then run the following inside any repo you want Gossamer to track:

```bash
entire enable
```

This hooks into your Git workflow so Gossamer can link AI sessions to commits.

### 2. Clone Gossamer and start it

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-org/gossamer.git
cd gossamer
npm run start
```

`npm run start` installs dependencies, builds, and launches both the backend server and the Electron desktop app together.

### 3. Add your repo in the Repos tab

Open Gossamer, go to the **Repos** tab, and add the local path to the repo you ran `entire enable` in. Gossamer will start indexing your sessions immediately.
